rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH VS. AMGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY D/B/A BERKSHIRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Moving Party In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF MAHER MEMARZADEH'S Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One. Off calendar as untimely filed and served. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Maher Memarzadeh?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Requests For Admission, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Moving Party In Support Of Motion To Compel Requests For Admission, Set One, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 1. PLAINTIFF MAHER MEMARZADEH'S Motion To Compel Requests For Admission, Set One. The motion is ordered off calendar as untimely filed and served. The motion was filed on December 11, 2025 and served by regular mail. A motion must be filed and served 16 court days before it is set for hearing, extended by five calendar days where it is served by regular mail on an address within California. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b).) The 16th court day after December 11, 2025, was January 6, 2026. The motion was set only one calendar day after the 16th court day. Defendant's counsel shall update their contact information in the court's register of actions if warranted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MORRISON VS. LAURA LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Answers, Without Objections To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories-General, Set One Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF DANIEL MORRISON'S Motion To Compel Answers, Without Objections To Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00. Plaintiff Daniel Morrison's motion to compel responses from defendant Laura Lei to specified form interrogatories, and for sanctions, is denied without prejudice. The court's tentative ruling in its entirety has been sent to counsel via email. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MORRISON VS. LAURA LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Answers, Without Objections To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set One Nos. 1-102 Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF DANIEL MORRISON'S Motion To Compel Answers, Without Objections To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set One Nos. 1-102 Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00. Plaintiff Daniel Morrison's motion to compel responses from defendant Laura Lei to his special interrogatories, and for sanctions, is denied without prejudice for the reasons stated in the companion order concerning the motion to compel responses to form interrogatories. Morrison fails to show he met and conferred prior to bringing the motion, and Morrison does not include a separate statement. The court orders Morrison to pay $1000 in sanctions to the court, and to file a notice that he has done so, within 30 days of this order. If Morrison contests the sanctions, the court will set the sanctions for a separate hearing so that Morrison has adequate notice and opportunity to be heard. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MORRISON VS. LAURA LEI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Plaintiff Daniel Morrison'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 5. PLAINTIFF DANIEL MORRISON'S Motion For Order Compelling Compliance With Plaintiff Daniel Morrison's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Against Defendant Laura Lei; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Laura Lei And Her Counsels, In The Amount Of $2,185.00. Plaintiff Daniel Morrison's motion to compel responses from defendant Laura Lei to his requests for production of documents, and for sanctions, is denied without prejudice. Morrison fails to show he met and conferred prior to bringing the motion, and Morrison does not include a separate statement. The court orders Morrison to pay $1000 in sanctions to the court, and to file a notice that he has done so, within 30 days of this order. As discussed more fully in the companion order on Morrison's motion to compel further responses to his form interrogatories, Morrison served the RFPs at issue on Lei, did not receive responses that he deemed timely, and did not receive a verification of the responses. He moved to compel further responses and verifications without engaging in meet and confer efforts. The same meet and confer requirement that applies to motions seeking further interrogatory responses also applies to motions seeking further RFP responses, by way of Code of Civil Procedure, section 2031.310, subdivision (b)(2). As in the companion motion, the court concludes that Morrison's failure to meet and confer lacks substantial justification, and Morrison is ordered to pay $1000 in sanctions to the court. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2023.010, subd. (i); id., section 2023.020; id., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h).) If Morrison contests the sanctions, the court will set the sanctions for a separate hearing so that Morrison has adequate notice and opportunity to be heard. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 7, 2026, line 9. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
